bullet_points: Load the CSV summary and JSON grid data.
Extract `cumulvec` and `tau_arr`, and compute the differential distribution $dN/d	au$ via numerical differentiation.
Calculate the fractional change $\Delta N_{abs} / N_{abs}$ relative to the CDM baseline ($f_{cool}=1, M_{cut}=0$).
Compute the structural sensitivity index $S = d \ln N_{abs} / d \ln M_{cut}$ at each redshift.
Tabulate and print the maximum fractional change due to cooling versus structure suppression.
Save all processed arrays and sensitivity metrics to a structured file for subsequent analysis.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Data processing and sensitivity quantification. Load the grid data, compute differential distributions, and quantify structural sensitivity.

bullet_points: Plot $N(>\tau)$ for selected redshifts and parameter combinations.
Generate a subplot showing the derivative $dN(>\tau)/dM_{cut}$ as a function of $\tau$ to identify sensitive bins.
Compute and plot the Kullback-Leibler divergence between $dN/d\tau$ at CDM and non-zero $M_{cut}$ values as a function of redshift.
Create a heatmap showing the fractional change in $N_{abs}$ as a function of $M_{cut}$ and $z$.
Save all plots as high-resolution PNG files.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Visualization and differential fingerprint analysis. Generate plots of the 21 cm forest signal and quantify distinguishability.

bullet_points: Implement the mapping functions for $\sigma_0/m_\chi$ based on the provided literature values for $n=0$ and $n=-4$ models.
Generate sensitivity curves showing detectable cross-sections for 10% and 20% changes in $N_{abs}$.
Save the resulting sensitivity data for the Fisher forecast step.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Cross-section mapping and sensitivity curve generation. Map $M_{cut}$ to physical DM-baryon parameters.

bullet_points: Incorporate a redshift-dependent source density $N_{los}(z) \propto (1+z)^{-\alpha}$ ($\alpha \approx 2-3$) and compute $N_{eff}(z) = N_{los}(z) \cdot x_{HI}(z)$.
Construct the full Fisher information matrix $F_{ij}$ using the $\tau$-bin sensitivities identified in Step 2.
Compute marginalized 1-$\sigma$ uncertainties on $M_{cut}$ after marginalizing over $J_{21}$ and $x_{HI}$ with Gaussian priors.
Print the full Fisher matrix, its inverse, and the marginalized uncertainties to the console.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Fisher forecast construction. Perform a rigorous Fisher analysis for the joint parameter set {M_{cut}, J_{21}, x_{HI}}.

bullet_points: Interpret the sensitivity results, highlighting the dominance of the structure suppression channel.
Compare derived 21 cm forest constraints with CMB and global 21 cm signal limits.
Discuss the uniqueness of the 21 cm forest in probing structure suppression.
Identify the optimal redshift window for future observations based on the Fisher analysis.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Comparative analysis and optimal window identification. Synthesize results and compare with existing constraints.

bullet_points: Summarize findings, referencing exact fractional suppression values, marginalized $\sigma(M_{cut})$, and $\sigma_0/m_\chi$ limits for $n=0$ and $n=-4$ models.
Provide a detailed discussion on the physical implications of the results.
Reference the generated plots and statistics to support the conclusions.
Finalize the report for the paper writer agents.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Final Results Report. Compile all quantitative findings, interpretations, and conclusions.
